text.tspan: Transformer-style organization: orthogonal routing, no arrow-text overlap, and the current mainline highlighted
Solid arrows: active mainline. Dashed arrows: fallback, ablation,
Paper-level takeaway: the bottleneck SemanticCrossAttn is the primary
style writer; the decoder adds texture; the color highway carries low-frequency appearance.
optional shallow / skip modulators exist in code
projects reference latent into bottleneck channel space
shallow style blocks and skip routing remain implemented,
but the main forward path passes gate = 0 on those routes
CrossAttnAdaGN exists here but is not active in the current mainline
content query × spatial style map key/value
with gate = 0, this behaves like a light content skip
writes local texture residuals using w
step_size and style_strength scale this update in integrate()
Training-only losses used by the current objective
The boxes below summarize what the implementation actually optimizes;
they are separated from the forward path so the main trunk stays readable.
unified SWD, or micro high-pass + macro low-pass SWD
cross-style repel when output stays too close
dashed: implemented but currently gated off in the main forward path
All loss boxes consume z_out; their comparison targets are z_s and/or z_c as listed inside each box.
Recommended paper narrative: explicit dual conditioning (reference latent or learned style prior),
bottleneck semantic painter as the primary style writer, decoder-side texture refinement, color highway, and residual latent update.
Reference-style conditioning is the default during training;
the learned prior is the fallback when no reference style latent is supplied.
Main flow is centered and purely vertical;
side routes use dedicated gutters so arrows never pass through labels.
